title: Rocky Statue Gets a VIP Move: Inside the Art Museum for the First Time!
summary: Do you know the movie Rocky? It's about a boxer who never gives up. There's a big bronze statue of Rocky Balboa with his fists in the air. For more than 20 years, it stood outside the Philadelphia Museum of Art. But now, for the first time ever, it has moved inside! This is part of a special exhibition called 'Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments.' It celebrates 50 years since the first Rocky movie came out. The exhibition has over 150 pieces of art, including some that are more than 2,000 years old! Some people used to argue that the Rocky statue wasn't real art because it was just a movie prop. But now it's inside a famous museum, so maybe they changed their minds. The museum says the statue is the most visited and photographed public artwork in Philadelphia. Almost as many people visit it as the Statue of Liberty! If you're in Philadelphia, you can see the statue inside the museum until August 2nd. Cool, right?
why_it_matters: It shows that something from a movie can become a famous piece of art that many people love to see, and you can visit it next time you go to Philadelphia.

background_read: The Rocky movies, starring Sylvester Stallone as Rocky Balboa, began in 1976.
The bronze statue was originally made as a movie prop and later put outside the Philadelphia Museum of Art.
It became one of the most visited landmarks in Philadelphia, almost as popular as the Statue of Liberty.
Article_Structure: WHO: The Rocky statue (of the movie character Rocky Balboa).
WHAT: It moved inside the Philadelphia Museum of Art for a special exhibition.
WHERE: Philadelphia Museum of Art in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
WHY: To be part of an exhibition called 'Rising Up: Rocky and the Making of Monuments' that celebrates 50 years of the Rocky movies.

perspectives.description: They are excited to see the statue inside a famous museum and celebrate the movie's anniversary.
They get to see a very popular statue up close and learn about how movies can create art.

perspectives.perspective: People who doubted it was art
perspectives.description: Some argued that the statue was just a movie prop, but now it is accepted as real art in a museum.
